authors: Al-Kaisy A, Palmisani S, Smith TE, Pang D, Lam K, Burgoyne W, Houghton R, Hudson E, Lucas J
title: 10 kHz High-Frequency Spinal Cord Stimulation for Chronic Axial Low Back Pain in Patients With No History of Spinal Surgery
study_question: Does 10 kHz HF-SCS improve chronic axial low back pain without prior spinal surgery?
population_assessed: Adults with chronic axial low back pain, no prior spinal surgery, medically refractory, unsuitable for surgery
outcome_measures: Back and leg VAS, ODI, EQ-5D, SF-36, opioid use, sleep, work status
inclusion_criteria: Age 18-65, axial low back pain 6+ months, VAS 5+, failed conservative treatment, no prior surgery
answer_to_study_question: HF10 reduced pain, disability, opioid use, and improved quality of life over 12 months
reasoning: Most fields showed near-complete agreement. Study design favored case_series_uncontrolled because title says open-label proof-of-concept and two models supported it; controlled cohort seemed unlikely.
field_level_decisions_json: {"title": "Used shorter consensus title version to stay within word limit while preserving core article identity.", "journal_name": "Standardized to Neuromodulation despite one expanded subtitle variant.", "study_design": "Rejected prospective_cohort_controlled; article appears prospective open-label uncontrolled.", "outcome_measures": "Merged common measures across models; excluded less consistently reported QALY.", "inclusion_criteria": "Used shared criteria reported by all models; omitted degenerative disc confirmation for brevity."}
